metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Male
Highly agreeable Agreeableness (92nd pct) — Cooperative, trusting, empathetic
Moderate Conscientiousness (48th pct)
Ambivert Extraversion (51st pct)
Moderate Neuroticism (42nd pct)
Conventional Openness to Experience (35th pct) — Conventional, practical, down-to-earth
  Very High Tender-Mindedness (94th pct) — Sympathetic, moved by others' needs
  Low Achievement Striving (38th pct) — Easygoing, content with modest achievement
  Moderate Self-Discipline (53rd pct)
  High Activity Level (61st pct) — Fast-paced, energetic, busy
  Moderate Excitement Seeking (55th pct)
  Moderate Positive Emotions (48th pct)
  Low Anger / Hostility (35th pct) — Slow to anger, easy-going
  Low Self-Consciousness (27th pct) — Comfortable socially, not easily embarrassed
  Moderate Neuroticism (51st pct)
  Moderate Chronological Age (45th pct)

attack_context.baseline_vs_goal: ALIGNED — baseline already points in the adversarial direction; reinforce it
attack_context.persuasion_goal: intensify and solidify an already negative opinion on Trust In Mainstream Journalism — push it further in the negative direction
attack_context.motivational_lever: intensify and solidify the existing position, supply confirmatory anecdotes, and harden the boundary against opposing arguments
attack_context.emotional_register: threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues
attack_context.issue_frame: information integrity and platforms, related policy debates, and societal impacts

message.reasoning: We are asked to estimate the post-exposure opinion score for a person with baseline -153 (moderate opposition to mainstream journalism) under an astroturf petition campaign (T2, weeks-long) aiming to decrease trust further (adversarial direction = -1, aligned goal). The profile: Male, 45, high agreeableness (especially trust, straightforwardness, altruism, tender-mindedness), moderate conscientiousness, low openness (conventional), moderate extraversion, low neuroticism (low anger/hostility, low self-consciousness). The person already leans negatively. The attack mechanism: social proof via fabricated consensus (petition astroturf) to intensify existing negative views. Given alignment, the campaign will likely reinforce and slightly deepen the existing opinion. The person is highly trusting (94.5% trust) and conventional (low openness), but also highly agreeable and compliant (81.6% compliance). This suggests susceptibility to social proof especially from perceived consensus. However, the person's low neuroticism and low self-consciousness may reduce anxiety-driven shifts. The attack is T2 campaign over weeks, not highly personalized. Since the goal is aligned, movement will be modest. The baseline is -153, already in opposition. I estimate a small reinforcement increase in negativity, say movement of about 20-30 points. Post score around -180. Confidence moderate due to mixed traits.
